problem_solved: Individuals with overweight and obesity (BMI ≥27) lack access to publicly-funded, evidence-based weight management programs. Many cannot afford private dietitian services and traditional weight loss programs often lack medical supervision and personalized support.
who_buys_it: Swedish regional healthcare authorities (landsting/regioner) that fund publicly-financed weight management treatment. Also accessible via employee wellness benefits (Benify, Epassi, Wellnet).
key_features: Digital dietitian consultations with medical assessment
BMI-based eligibility screening (minimum BMI 27 for publicly-funded treatment)
Integration with Swedish healthcare system for tax-funded treatment access
Mobile app for ongoing support and tracking
business_model: B2B/B2G model. Publicly-funded through Swedish regional healthcare systems for eligible patients (BMI ≥27). Also available through corporate wellness benefit platforms for employees.
why_varden_should_care: Swedish digital health company with reimbursement pathway through public healthcare system. Addresses obesity/overweight which is a major healthcare cost driver. Platform model with both B2G (public funding) and B2B2C (wellness benefits) revenue streams. Scalable to other Nordic countries with similar healthcare structures.

problem_solved: Dietitians and clinical nutritionists in Swedish healthcare settings need reliable, Swedish-language software for nutritional calculations, meal planning, and patient dietary assessment. Existing solutions often lack local food databases and Swedish regulatory compliance.
who_buys_it: Clinical dietitians in Swedish hospitals, private practice nutritionists, municipal healthcare facilities, and nutrition researchers in Sweden.
key_features: Swedish food database with local ingredients and products
Integration with Swedish healthcare IT systems
business_model: SaaS subscription model. Pricing likely tiered based on institution size (hospitals vs. private practice). Local Swedish support and training.
why_varden_should_care: Established Swedish clinical nutrition software with local market fit. Addresses specific need for Swedish-language tools and local food data. Potential for expansion to other Nordic countries (Norway, Denmark, Finland) with localized versions. Part of KostData ecosystem which suggests deeper market penetration.
